# Stream Runtime Vectors

These vectors characterize platform-neutral stream lifecycle behavior before stream utilities or facade stream orchestration move into shared. `StreamRuntimePolicyCommonTest.kt` consumes ordered-emission, terminal-error, checked and unchecked connection-guard, consumer-cancellation, disconnect-after-subscription, duplicate-completion, and late-emission-after-completion policies as executable shared planning, while Android channel utility tests and iOS `StreamContinuationListTest.swift` remain the authoritative platform characterizations until production stream utilities are delegated to shared code.

`stream-runtime-readiness.json` names the shared-contract behavior-family gate for generic stream runtime extraction: all stream policy vectors including `ordered-emissions-policy.json`, terminal-error propagation and observer cleanup, checked and unchecked connection guards, consumer cancellation cleanup/upstream cancellation/idempotence, disconnect-after-subscription terminal cleanup and platform mapping, duplicate completion idempotence, post-completion emission suppression, active observer count cleanup, platform stream vector references, and the `compile-verification-gate` must remain covered before production stream lifecycle code moves into shared.
